chapter: ENG9_6 – Weathering the Storm in Ersama
title: Weathering the Storm in Ersama – Easy SAQs

questions.question: Who was Prashant?
questions.answer: Prashant was a nineteen-year-old boy who helped the villagers during the cyclone.

questions.question: Where had Prashant gone on 27 October 1999?
questions.answer: He had gone to Ersama to spend the day with a friend.

questions.question: What natural disaster struck Orissa in the story?
questions.answer: A super cyclone struck Orissa.

questions.question: How fast were the cyclone winds blowing?
questions.answer: The cyclone winds were blowing at about 350 km per hour.

questions.question: Where did Prashant and the family take shelter?
questions.answer: They took shelter on the roof of the house.

questions.question: What helped them survive without food for several days?
questions.answer: Tender coconuts from fallen coconut trees helped them survive.

questions.question: Why did Prashant want to return to his village?
questions.answer: He wanted to know whether his family had survived the cyclone.

questions.question: How far was Prashant’s village from Ersama?
questions.answer: His village was about eighteen kilometres away from Ersama.

questions.question: What did Prashant carry while travelling through floodwaters?
questions.answer: He carried a long sturdy stick.

questions.question: What shocking sights did Prashant see on his journey?
questions.answer: He saw dead bodies of people and animals floating in the water.

questions.question: What was the name of Prashant’s village?
questions.answer: The name of his village was Kalikuda.

questions.question: Where did Prashant find his family?
questions.answer: He found his family at the Red Cross shelter.

questions.question: How many houses were destroyed in the village?
questions.answer: Ninety-six houses were washed away.

questions.question: How many people were staying in the shelter?
questions.answer: About 2500 people were staying in the shelter.

questions.question: What did Prashant organise to get food for the villagers?
questions.answer: He organised a group of youths and elders to pressurise a merchant for rice.

questions.question: How was the rice cooked in the shelter?
questions.answer: It was cooked on a fire made from branches of fallen trees.

questions.question: Who dropped food parcels for the villagers?
questions.answer: A military helicopter dropped food parcels.

questions.question: How did the children communicate with the helicopters?
questions.answer: They lay on the sand with utensils on their stomachs to signal hunger.

questions.question: Why were many children orphaned?
questions.answer: Their parents had died in the cyclone.

questions.question: How did Prashant help orphaned children?
questions.answer: He arranged shelter and care for them within the community.

questions.question: What programme did Prashant encourage women to join?
questions.answer: He encouraged them to join the food-for-work programme.

questions.question: What activity did Prashant organise for children?
questions.answer: He organised cricket matches for them.

questions.question: Why did Prashant oppose sending orphans to institutions?
questions.answer: Because children needed love and care within the community.

questions.question: What qualities of Prashant are shown in the story?
questions.answer: He shows courage, leadership, and compassion.

questions.question: What is the main message of the story?
questions.answer: The story shows the importance of courage, leadership, and community cooperation during disasters.
